args.task:
Perform comprehensive subdomain enumeration and takeover checks for the target domain scaa.us.
Use passive sources (certificate transparency, crt.sh, public OSINT) and active tools (subfinder). Perform DNS bruteforce and permutation discovery against common wordlists. Probe discovered hosts with httpx to identify live HTTP/HTTPS services, ports, response codes, and server headers. Detect wildcard DNS, unresolved patterns, dangling CNAMEs, and potential subdomain takeover candidates. Produce machine-readable output (JSON) listing subdomain, resolved IPs, protocol, port, status, and notes with a prioritized takeover candidate list.
args.name: Subdomain Enumerator

args.task:
Perform comprehensive port and service discovery for scaa.us and any discovered subdomains. 
Use naabu for high-speed TCP discovery (scan ports 1-65535) and include UDP probes where feasible. 
Follow up live hosts with nmap (-p- -sV -sC --version-all) for service/version detection and banner grabbing. 
Probe any HTTP/HTTPS services with httpx to capture status codes, server headers, TLS versions, redirect chains, and common management/admin panel fingerprints. 
Output machine-readable JSON with: host, resolved IPs, open ports, transport (tcp/udp), service name, version/banner, protocol (http/https), status_code, server_header, tls_info, and notes. 
Flag and prioritize likely management interfaces, exposed control panels, and unusual services for follow-up testing.
args.name: Port & Service Discovery

args.task:
Crawl http://scaa.us thoroughly to map the web application and extract client-side attack surface for dynamic testing.

Actions to perform:
- Fetch robots.txt, sitemap.xml, /.well-known/* and common discovery paths
- Recursively spider same-origin pages (follow links/forms) to depth ~5, capturing URLs, parameters, form fields, cookies, and response codes
- Download all JavaScript assets (inline and external). Parse JS for:
  - XHR/fetch/axios/webpacked endpoints and route patterns
  - Hard-coded keys, tokens, secrets, API hosts, and suspicious comment text
  - Client-side routing/SPA framework indicators (Next.js, React Router, Vue, etc.)
  - Dynamic endpoints and callback URLs used by the frontend
- Identify Service Workers, WebSockets endpoints, and any exposed admin/dev/debug pages
- Record CSP, security headers, cookie flags, and redirect chains observed
- Produce machine-readable artifacts: sitemap.json, endpoints.json (with method/parameters where found), js_files_list.txt, js_secrets.json (flagged potential secrets with confidence), and a prioritized list of client-side targets for follow-up testing (APIs, auth endpoints, webhook/callback URLs)

Deliverables:
- Full crawl output with metadata (URL, status, content-type, response size, discovery path)
- Extracted API endpoints and parameters usable for automated attack-phase agents
- Prioritized notes for interesting targets (e.g., auth APIs, upload/preview endpoints, internal API hostnames found in JS)

Handle errors robustly and continue crawling; include any rate-limiting or anti-bot behavior observed in notes.
args.name: Web Crawler & JS Recon

args.command:
OUTDIR=/workspace/scans/scaa
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"
cd "$OUTDIR"
echo "[*] Resolving scaa.us"
dig +short scaa.us | tee ips.txt || true

echo "[*] Running subfinder (passive+active subdomain enumeration)"
subfinder -d scaa.us -silent -o subdomains.txt || true

# Ensure scaa.us is included and dedupe
if [ ! -s subdomains.txt ]; then echo "scaa.us" > subdomains.txt; fi
grep -qxF "scaa.us" subdomains.txt || echo "scaa.us" >> subdomains.txt
sort -u subdomains.txt -o subdomains.txt || true
echo "[*] Found subdomains:"
cat subdomains.txt || true

echo "[*] Probing hosts with httpx (quick liveness + headers + TLS probe)"
httpx -l subdomains.txt -silent -sc -server -title -tls-probe -t 25 -timeout 10 -retries 1 -json -o httpx_raw.json || true

echo "[*] Starting background full naabu (1-65535). Output: naabu_full.jsonl, log: naabu_full.log"
nohup naabu -list subdomains.txt -p 1-65535 -scan-type c -Pn -rate 300 -c 50 -timeout 1000 -retries 1 -verify -silent -j -o naabu_full.jsonl > naabu_full.log 2>&1 & echo $! > naabu_full.pid || true

echo "[*] Running quick naabu top-ports 100 for fast feedback"
naabu -list subdomains.txt -top-ports 100 -scan-type c -Pn -rate 200 -c 50 -timeout 1000 -retries 1 -verify -silent -j -o naabu_quick.jsonl || true

echo "[*] Preparing and running targeted nmap service/version checks for discovered quick-open ports (timeout per host: 45s)"
python3 - <<'PY'
import json,os,subprocess
hosts_ports={}
try:
  with open('naabu_quick.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
      try:
        j=json.loads(line)
      except:
        continue
      host=j.get('host')
      port=j.get('port')
      if host and port:
        hosts_ports.setdefault(host,[]).append(str(port))
except FileNotFoundError:
  pass

for host, ports in hosts_ports.items():
  ports_s=','.join(sorted(set(ports)))
  safe_host = host.replace('/','_').replace(':','_')
  fname='nmap_'+safe_host
  cmd=['nmap','-n','-Pn','-sV','-sC','-p',ports_s,'--version-all','-oA',fname,host]
  print('RUNNING:', ' '.join(cmd))
  try:
    subprocess.run(cmd, timeout=45, check=False)
  except Exception as e:
    print('nmap for',host,'failed or timed out:',e)

# Build minimal JSON summary combining hosts and httpx hits
summary=[]
try:
  with open('httpx_raw.json') as f:
    for line in f:
      line=line.strip()
      if not line: continue
      try:
        summary.append(json.loads(line))
      except:
        pass
except FileNotFoundError:
  pass

out={'hosts_with_quick_ports': list(hosts_ports.keys()), 'httpx': summary, 'note':'Full naabu (1-65535) running in background: naabu_full.jsonl, log: naabu_full.log'}
with open('scan_summary.json','w') as fh:
  json.dump(out, fh, indent=2)
print('WROTE scan_summary.json')
PY

echo "[*] Scan summary (scan_summary.json):"
cat scan_summary.json || true

echo "[*] Quick scan finished. Full port scan continues in background (naabu_full.pid / naabu_full.log / naabu_full.jsonl).
